mnemonics.title: ESP Header Fields
mnemonics.content: SPy SPy PANDA: SPI, Sequence, Payload, Padding, Pad Length, Next Header, Authentication Data. Remember the header structure sequence.

mnemonics.title: Modes of Operation
mnemonics.content: TRUCK vs TUNNEL: Transport mode carries original IP header (like a truck carrying visible goods), Tunnel mode wraps in new IP header (like goods hidden in a tunnel)

mnemonics.title: ESP Trailer Fields
mnemonics.content: PPN: Padding → Pad Length → Next Header. Think 'Please Pass Notes' for trailer components

mnemonics.title: SA Bundles
mnemonics.content: SAD BEE: Security Association Database entries need Bundle of Encryption & Authentication, Either order (Encrypt-then-Auth or Auth-then-Encrypt)

acronyms.usage: Identifies the Security Association for packet processing
Remember this handles SA establishment for ESP
Used in ESP's authentication data field

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a secure envelope: Header = address section, Payload = sealed letter, Trailer = wax stamp + seal
Picture two roads: Transport mode = original car with armor plating, Tunnel mode = car inside an armored tunnel vehicle
Think of package tracking numbers - each ESP packet gets unique sequential ID to prevent duplicates
Imagine a passport - SPI is the passport number, SA contains all security 'visa' requirements

analogies.analogy: Like a bank vault transport: Data is encrypted (locked in vault), authenticated (security seals), and tracked (sequence numbers like tracking IDs)
Similar to a locker number at train station - tells receiver which key (SA parameters) to use for opening
Like adding bubble wrap to fill a box completely - ensures payload fits encryption algorithm's block size
Like a multi-lock security door: One SA is deadbolt (encryption), another is door alarm (authentication)
